user_message: Can you extract the account health from this support ticket for Iron Creek Mining? There's been no activity for 45 days and I'm worried.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: account health Discussion - Iron Creek Mining
Date: 2024-01-17
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is account health.

[00:07:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:12:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:15:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:19:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:24:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:26:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:31:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

handoffs.condition: ECIF workscope or partner engagement warranted

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF49-M trap (Partner-evaluation module assumes single-partner when workshop involved multiple); produces complete Workshop recap, ECIF workscope draft (if warranted), partner plan, follow-up items with owners, mile
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF49-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF49-M failure mode (Partner-evaluation module assumes single-partner when workshop involved multiple)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF49-M -- Partner-evaluation module assumes single-partner when workshop involved multiple
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
